rulings.Case Title: HOUSING RIGHTS COMMITTEE OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. VIC YANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Plainitffs To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant Vic Yang And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Vic Yang
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 17, 2016, Line 3. PLAINTIFFS HOUSING RIGHTS COMMITTEE OF SAN FRANCISCO, UCHIDA STEVEN'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant Vic Yang And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Vic Yang. GRANTED. Plaintiff's counsel and Mr. Yang or his counsel are required to meet and confer regarding a mutually acceptable date for the deposition to take place in person not later than May 30, 2016. Defendant has indicated that he is available for deposition in person May 19-24, 2016. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED, as it appears that defendant Vic Yang may have been traveling with limited access to email during his previously scheduled deposition, and that additional efforts to meet and confer could have obviated the need for plaintiff's motion. Plaintiff shall submit a proposed order. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL ROUPPET VS. FERGUS O'SULLIVAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 17, 2016, Line 4. DEFENDANTS FERGUS O'SULLIVAN, FOS TEAM, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DENIED, as moot. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL ROUPPET VS. FERGUS O'SULLIVAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 17, 2016, Line 4. DEFENDANTS FERGUS O'SULLIVAN, FOS TEAM, INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff's Fourth Cause of action is barred by the statute of limitations because it was pled more than three years after the cause of action accrued. Plaintiff's First, Second, Third and Fifth Causes of Action are also SUSTAINED without leave to amend because Plaintiff has failed to plead facts establishing that any misrepresentation has been made related to the lockout, and has failed to plead facts that establish a written contract not barred by the Statute of Limitations. =(501/REQ)
